title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement s.nolte@dshs-koeln.de. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing to the c3dr package
text: Think spiro useful? Let others discover , telling person, via Twitter blog post. Using spiro paper writing? Retrieve citation information running citation("spiro") R console.

title: Contributing to the c3dr package
text: Using spiro got stuck? Browse documentation see can find solution. Still stuck? Post question issue GitHub. Want ask question private? Contact package maintainer mail.

title: Contributing to the c3dr package
text: idea new spiro feature? Take look documentation issue list see isn’t included suggested yet. , suggest idea issue GitHub. can’t promise implement idea, helps : Explain detail work. Keep scope narrow possible. See want contribute code idea well.

title: Contributing to the c3dr package
text: try follow GitHub flow development. Fork repo clone computer. learn process, see guide. forked cloned project since worked , pull changes original repo clone using git pull upstream master. Open RStudio project file (.Rproj). Write code. Test code (bonus points adding unit tests). Document code (see function documentation ). Check code devtools::check() aim 0 errors warnings. Commit push changes. Submit pull request.

code: @Manual{,   title = {c3dr: Work with motion capture data in R},   author = {Simon Nolte},   year = {2024},   url = {https://github.com/smnnlt/c3dr},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000}, }

title: Work with motion capture data in R
text: R package working motion capture data based C++ library EZC3D.

title: Work with motion capture data in R
text: C3D (https://www.c3d.org/) file format biomechanical data, namely motion capture recording additional analog data (e.g., force plates electromyography). c3dr package allow read analyze C3D files R (https://www.r-project.org/.html), programming language popular statistical analyses data visualization. build open-source C++ library EZC3D (https://github.com/pyomeca/ezc3d).

title: Work with motion capture data in R
text: Install c3dr GitHub:

title: Work with motion capture data in R
code: library(c3dr)  # get example data. Insert your file path instead, e.g. # path <- "folder/myfilename.c3d" path <- c3d_example()   # import data d <- c3d_read(path) d #> A c3d object with #> - 54 data points and 1885 frames #> - 7.85 s measurement duration (240 fps) #> - 1 analog channels (960 fps)  # structure of the imported object str(d, max.level = 1) #> List of 6 #>  $ header   :List of 6 #>  $ labels   : chr [1:54] "LASI" "RASI" "LPSI" "RPSI" ... #>  $ alabels  : chr "Electric Potential.Trigger" #>  $ data     :List of 1885 #>  $ residuals: num [1:1885, 1:54] 172 171 172 172 172 ... #>  $ analog   :List of 1885 #>  - attr(*, "class")= chr [1:2] "c3d" "list"  # read point data p <- c3d_data(d) p[1:5, 1:5] #>     LASI_x   LASI_y   LASI_z   RASI_x   RASI_y #> 1 514.7515 426.1067 1111.939 517.2385 140.1397 #> 2 514.1281 425.5054 1112.686 517.2141 140.0569 #> 3 514.5764 425.8453 1112.585 517.1833 139.9610 #> 4 514.4996 425.7862 1112.925 517.1443 139.9025 #> 5 514.3743 425.7397 1113.257 517.0745 139.8348  # alternative long data format p_long <- c3d_data(d, format = "long") p_long[1:5, 1:5] #>     frame type      LASI      RASI      LPSI #> 1.x     1    x  514.7515  517.2385  365.6084 #> 1.y     1    y  426.1067  140.1397  348.0317 #> 1.z     1    z 1111.9390 1085.5681 1196.8900 #> 2.x     2    x  514.1281  517.2141  365.5312 #> 2.y     2    y  425.5054  140.0569  347.8595  # read analog data a <- c3d_analog(d) head(a) #>   Electric Potential.Trigger #> 1              -0.0001525879 #> 2              -0.0001525879 #> 3               0.0000000000 #> 4               0.0000000000 #> 5               0.0000000000 #> 6               0.0003051758

title: Get analog data from a c3d file — c3d_analog
Get analog data from a c3d file — c3d_analog
text: x c3d object, imported c3d_read().

title: Get analog data from a c3d file — c3d_analog
text: data.frame n rows m columns, n number frames recorded times number analog subframes per frame, m number recorded analog channels.

title: Get analog data from a c3d file — c3d_analog
code: # Import example data d <- c3d_read(c3d_example())  # get analog data a <- c3d_analog(d) head(a) #>   Electric Potential.Trigger #> 1              -0.0001525879 #> 2              -0.0001525879 #> 3               0.0000000000 #> 4               0.0000000000 #> 5               0.0000000000 #> 6               0.0003051758

title: Get point data from a c3d file — c3d_data

title: Get point data from a c3d file — c3d_data
Get point data from a c3d file — c3d_data
text: x c3d object, imported c3d_read(). format Either "wide" (default), "long", "longest" determine format resulting data frame. wide format Long format three columns per point (x,y,z), long format three rows per frame per point (x,y,z). longest format one row per data entry.

title: Get point data from a c3d file — c3d_data
text: data.frame c3d point data. structure data frame depends 'format' argument.

title: Get point data from a c3d file — c3d_data

title: Convert wide to longest data for c3d points — c3d_longest

text: x data.frame c3d point data three columns (x, y, z) per point.

title: Convert wide to longest data for c3d points — c3d_longest
text: data.frame one data column. 3\*n\*k rows, n number recorded frames k number recorded points.

title: Convert wide to longest data for c3d points — c3d_longest
code: # Import example data d <- c3d_read(c3d_example()) # get point data in wide format w <- c3d_data(d, format = "wide") # convert to long data ll <- c3d_longest(w) head(ll) #>        frame type point     value #> 1.LASI     1    x  LASI  514.7515 #> 2.LASI     1    y  LASI  426.1067 #> 3.LASI     1    z  LASI 1111.9390 #> 1.RASI     1    x  RASI  517.2385 #> 2.RASI     1    y  RASI  140.1397 #> 3.RASI     1    z  RASI 1085.5681
